title: 7. Avatars and MuseTalk
description: How avatar engines, MuseTalk runtime settings, avatar packs, emotions, and preview/focus controls fit together.

steps.title: Avatar Engines
steps.body: Avatar Engine controls whether NC runs without an avatar, uses MuseTalk, or uses another installed avatar provider. Start with None until chat and speech are confirmed.

steps.title: MuseTalk Needs a Pack
steps.body: MuseTalk needs a prepared avatar pack. A pack can include several emotion variants, such as neutral, angry, and sad.

steps.title: VRAM Mode
steps.body: VRAM mode trades quality and speed against memory use. Very Low VRAM is the safest first choice; higher modes should be tested later.

steps.title: Emotion Availability
steps.body: Personas should ask for emotions that actually exist in the selected avatar pack. If the pack only has neutral, angry, and sad, keep presets within those tags.

steps.title: Preview and Focus
steps.body: Preview opens the avatar runtime view for checking output. Avatar Focus is for a more presentation-like view once the pipeline is stable.

steps.title: Test After Each Change
steps.body: Avatar changes touch heavier runtime code. Change one thing at a time, start a short chat reply, then confirm that speech, lip sync, and emotion selection still behave.
